description_long: This is a decorative urban pennant mounted on a vertical pole with a tripod base for stability. The pole is topped with a spear-like finial and features a horizontal crossbar from which the pennant hangs. The pennant itself is a deep blue fabric bordered with gold trim and adorned with stylized gold emblems, possibly representing a crest or insignia. The fabric is attached to the crossbar via loops, and a gold band secures the pennant to the pole at its midpoint. The base is metallic and sturdy, designed to keep the pennant upright in outdoor environments. The overall construction suggests durability and ceremonial or decorative use in public spaces such as plazas, parks, or near civic buildings.
description: A blue and gold ceremonial pennant with stylized emblems, mounted on a spear-tipped pole with a tripod base. The pennant hangs from a horizontal crossbar and is secured with gold trim and bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue pennant with gold trim and emblems, clearly displaying the decorative design and attachment loops.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the pole and pennant, with the fabric barely visible and the spear tip and tripod base in silhouette.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the back of the pennant and the structure of the pole and crossbar.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the pole and the tripod base, with minimal visibility of the pennant fabric.
attribute_graphic_design: The pennant features a deep blue background with gold trim and two stylized gold emblems, one at the top and one at the bottom. The design is symmetrical and likely represents a crest or symbolic motif.
